info.title: whisper-wrap engine API
info.description: HTTP/WebSocket/SSE API for the whisper-wrap transcription engine.

200.description: API discovery document — a hand-maintained list of top-level routes (ad-hoc JSON). Token-exempt.
The prompt-action registry (categories + actions) as ad-hoc JSON.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description:
Optional per-call model override (llm-provider-abstraction). Absent
selects the active provider's default model.

requestBody.description: Either a JSON body `{"text": "…"}` for a text question, or raw/multipart audio (`audio/*`, `application/octet-stream`, or `multipart/form-data` with a `file` part) to transcribe-then-ask.

200.description: Answer. When `stream=true` the response is a `text/event-stream` (SSE) of incremental `data:` chunks terminated by a final event; otherwise a single JSON answer object.

503.description: No ASR model loaded (audio input) or no LLM provider configured.

200.description: Auxiliary (diarization + VAD) models with install state (ad-hoc JSON).

post.summary:
Start (or report an already-running) download. Returns immediately; poll
GET /aux-models/download/{id}.

requestBody.description: The auxiliary model id to download.

200.description: Download started/queued (ad-hoc JSON with a progress handle).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Auxiliary model id whose download progress to read.

200.description: Current download progress (ad-hoc JSON).

delete.summary:
Cancel an in-flight aux download (mirror of models::cancel_download, keyed
by aux id). The worker notices the flag between chunks.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Auxiliary model id whose download to cancel.

200.description: Download cancelled (ad-hoc JSON).

404.description: No active download for that auxiliary model.

delete.summary: Uninstall an aux model (delete its ONNX file from disk).

200.description: Auxiliary model weights removed (ad-hoc JSON).

get.summary: `GET /config/ai` — masked read of the active config.

200.description: The masked, read-safe AI provider config.

put.summary:
`PUT /config/ai` — validate, save, swap the live client, return the masked
view. Invalid provider -> 400. Empty `apiKey` keeps the stored key.

requestBody.description: Partial AI provider config update (camelCase). Unknown keys are ignored; the runtime accepts a lenient JSON object.

200.description: The updated masked config.

get.summary:
`GET /config/ai/models` — list provider models using the SUBMITTED
provider/base-url/key. Any fetch failure -> 200 with `models: []` + a
non-null `error` (never a 5xx).

200.description: Available models for the given provider/baseUrl/apiKey, or an error descriptor embedded in the JSON.

post.summary:
`POST /config/ai/test` — build a transient client from the submitted body,
do one minimal non-streaming `ask`, report `{ ok, error }`. Never persists.

200.description: Connectivity test result `{ok, …}` (failures are reported in the body, not as a non-200 status).

post.summary:
`POST /items/{id}/ai?model=` — run a prompt over the item's transcript (D5).
Hard DAG gate: the item must exist (404) and have a transcript (409); the
LLM must be configured (503). `?model=` is recorded but does not switch the
LLM until llm-provider-abstraction.

requestBody.description: The prompt to run against the item's transcript.

409.description: The item has no transcript yet, or an AI run is already in progress.

post.summary:
`POST /items/{id}/diarize?quality=` — diarize the item's stored audio (D4).
When the item has a transcript the diarization is merged into
speaker-attributed segments; otherwise the raw speaker turns are recorded.

404.description: No item or stored audio for that id.

409.description: A diarization run is already in progress for the item.

get.summary:
`GET /items/{id}/runs` — every run recorded against an item (oldest first),
each with its job-status contract + result snapshot. The run inspector
(fe-item-detail-runs) reads this to show the re-runnable pipeline history.
An item with no runs returns an empty list (not a 404).

200.description: All runs for the item (oldest first) as `{ "runs": RunRecord[] }`; an empty list when the item has no runs.

post.summary:
`POST /items/{id}/transcribe?model=` — transcribe the item's stored audio on
the chosen model, snapshotting the transcript into a transcribe run (D3).

202.description: Transcription run accepted — returns the run descriptor; poll `GET /runs/{id}`.

404.description: No item or stored audio for that id.

409.description: A transcription run is already in progress for the item.

get.description: WebSocket endpoint for live captioning. The client performs a WebSocket upgrade, then streams 16 kHz mono `pcm_s16le` audio as binary frames. The server emits JSON text messages: `{"type":"partial"|"final","text","start_ms","end_ms"}`, `{"type":"warning","message":...}`, or `{"type":"error","message":...}` followed by close code 1003. OpenAPI 3.1 cannot model the bidirectional frame protocol, so this entry is descriptive only and declares no request or response body schema.

101.description: Switching Protocols — the connection is upgraded to WebSocket.

200.description: Installed + registered ASR models with active/loaded state (ad-hoc JSON).

requestBody.description: The model name to load as the active engine.

200.description: Model activated (ad-hoc JSON status).

post.summary:
Start (or report an already-running) download of a model's ggml
weights. Returns immediately; poll GET /models/download/{name}.

200.description: Download started/queued (ad-hoc JSON with a progress handle).

409.description: A download for this model is already in progress.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Model name whose download progress to read.

200.description: Current download progress (ad-hoc JSON).

404.description: No active download for that model.

delete.summary:
Request cancellation of an in-flight download. The worker notices the
flag between chunks, removes the partial file, then flips the job to
"cancelled" — poll GET /models/download/{name} to observe it land.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Model name whose download to cancel.

200.description: Download cancelled (ad-hoc JSON).

404.description: No active download for that model.

delete.summary:
Uninstall a model's on-disk weights (the "D" in model CRUD). Refuses to
remove the currently-loaded model — switch away first.

200.description: Model weights removed (ad-hoc JSON).

409.description: Model weights missing or cannot be removed.

get.summary:
`GET /runs/{id}` (D5): the job-status contract for any run, served from the
persisted row — so it answers correctly after a restart, when no in-memory
job remains. An unknown id is a 404 with the standard error envelope.

200.description: The run's job-status contract plus result snapshot.
Engine health + active-model snapshot (ad-hoc JSON). Token-exempt.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description:
Optional per-request ASR model (per-request-asr-model). Absent selects
the active engine; present selects that model with no global swap.

requestBody.description: Audio payload — raw bytes with an `audio/*` (or `application/octet-stream`) Content-Type, or `multipart/form-data` with a `file` part.

200.description: Transcription result — text plus timing/metadata (ad-hoc JSON object).

400.description: Empty or unreadable audio body, or missing multipart `file` field.

413.description: Audio exceeds the configured maximum file size.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description:
Optional per-request ASR model (stage-run-endpoints D7). Absent selects
the active engine — the v2 behavior.

requestBody.description: Multipart upload with a `file` part carrying the meeting audio. Query params select the filename, diarization quality tier (`fast`|`balanced`), and optional per-request ASR model.

202.description: Job accepted — returns a job descriptor `{id, status, …}`; poll `GET /transcribe/meeting/{id}` for progress.

400.description: Invalid quality tier or malformed upload (ad-hoc `{detail:{error,reason}}` body).

413.description: Audio exceeds the configured maximum file size.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Meeting job id returned by the submit call.

200.description: Current job status and, when finished, the result (ad-hoc JSON).

202.description: Cancellation requested for an in-flight job.

requestBody.description: OpenAI-compatible transcription request — `multipart/form-data` with a `file` part (audio) plus optional `model`/`language`/`response_format` fields.

200.description: Transcription in OpenAI JSON shape (e.g. `{"text": …}`).

413.description: Audio exceeds the configured maximum file size.

requestBody.description: OpenAI-compatible translation request (transcribe + translate to English) — `multipart/form-data` with a `file` part plus optional `model`/`response_format` fields.

200.description: English translation in OpenAI JSON shape (e.g. `{"text": …}`).

413.description: Audio exceeds the configured maximum file size.

200.description: Paged meeting list with item-metadata filters applied (ad-hoc JSON).
Meeting created (ad-hoc JSON with the new id).
The meeting with its items (ad-hoc JSON).
Meeting deleted.
Updated meeting (ad-hoc JSON).
The stored audio blob (binary, original media type).

404.description: No meeting or no stored audio for that id.

requestBody.description: Multipart upload carrying the meeting's audio blob in a `file` part.

200.description: Audio stored (ad-hoc JSON).
OpenAI-compatible model list `{object:"list", data:[…]}` describing the active ASR model.
Paged session list with item-metadata filters applied (ad-hoc JSON).
Session created (ad-hoc JSON with the new id).
Cleared stored audio blobs across sessions (ad-hoc JSON summary).

get.description: Server-Sent Events stream of session-list changes. On connect the server emits a `ready` event, then a `changed` event whenever any session is created, updated, or deleted; comment heartbeats keep idle connections alive. Clients re-fetch `GET /v1/sessions` on each `changed`.

200.description: An SSE stream of `ready` then `changed` events.
The session with its items and finals (ad-hoc JSON).
Session deleted.
Updated session (ad-hoc JSON).
The stored audio blob (binary, original media type).

404.description: No session or no stored audio for that id.

requestBody.description: Multipart upload carrying the session's audio blob in a `file` part.

200.description: Audio stored (ad-hoc JSON).

requestBody.description: A finalized transcript segment to append to the session.

200.description: Segment appended.

AiConfigUpdate.description: Update body for `PUT /config/ai`. camelCase on the wire.

AiConfigView.description: The masked, read-safe view of the active config. Wire shape is camelCase.

ApiErrorBody.description:
The `{ "detail": string }` body every [`ApiError`] serializes to. `ApiError`
itself is private and only implements `IntoResponse`, so it cannot derive
`ToSchema`; this is the single reusable schema every fallible operation
references for its non-200 error responses (rather than re-inlining the
shape per handler).

RunKind.description:
The operation a run records. The `runs` table is the eventual superset of
all three kinds; this change writes only `Diarize` (the meeting pipeline).

RunOrigin.description:
Provenance of a run in the item listing (unify-run-ledger). `Stage` is a
real row in the `runs` ledger; `Capture` and `Legacy` are read-only runs
synthesized at list time from a session's `finals` / legacy `action_runs`,
so a read surface sees one unified history without reconciling the storage
split. Synthesized runs are never re-runnable.

RunRecord.description:
The job-status contract JSON returned by `GET /runs/{id}`. `params` is
stored in the table (D1) but deliberately NOT part of the status contract,
so it does not appear here.

origin.description:
Provenance (unify-run-ledger): `stage` for a real ledger row,
`capture`/`legacy` for a run synthesized at list time. Ledger reads are
always `stage`; the synthesizers set the other two.

result.description:
The run's immutable result snapshot (stage-run-endpoints, D8), parsed
from `result_json`. Serialized as `result`: null when the run has no
snapshot. Additive to the job-status contract.

RunStatus.description: The five lifecycle states every run reports (job-status contract).
